Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-14_19-17-31_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/game-of-life.ts

The test should verify Rule 4: a dead cell with exactly 3 live neighbors becomes alive.
Use the example from prompt.md: Input [(0,0),(1,0),(0,1)] (an L-shape). The dead cell (1,1) has exactly 3 live neighbors at (0,0),(1,0),(0,1) → should become alive.
Note: After applying all rules, that input also produces (0,0),(1,0),(0,1) becoming alive again because they each have 2 neighbors (survival), plus (1,1) (reproduction). To isolate the reproduction rule cleanly, you may want to assert that the result CONTAINS [1,1] (i.e., the dead cell with 3 neighbors comes alive). Use `expect(result).toContainEqual([1, 1])` or check membership via a Set, to keep this test focused on the reproduction behavior alone.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Green after completion.
You MUST output the full Step 7 block verbatim (both Compilation
Prediction and Runtime Prediction lines, each with Correct or
Incorrect). Do not abbreviate. Do not collapse the two prediction
lines into one.
